input: Perform a complete memory forensics investigation of the Windows system image at /home/ubuntu/Downloads/Standard-Forensic_Case/Rocba-Memory/Rocba-Memory.raw. Enumerate all running and hidden processes, map parent-child relationships, examine process command lines and loaded modules, review network connections for anomalies, identify any regions of injected or unsigned code, and compare results against a known-good baseline if one is available. Write all findings and plugin output to ./analysis/memory/ under the case workdir.

The hunt workstation from the SRL-2018 network simulation is suspected of running a persistent implant. Memory captures for all machines are stored as compressed archives under /home/ubuntu/Downloads/SRL-2018/SRL-2018/. Locate the hunt workstation capture, extract it, and triage for evidence of process injection, anomalous network connections, and hidden or exited processes.

Locate a Windows memory image in /home/ubuntu/Downloads/Standard-Forensic-Case-2/ and analyse it for indicators of a lateral movement event, including injected threads and anomalous network connections. If the requested artifact does not exist in that folder, reply with a single line beginning "ABSENT:" describing what you searched for, and perform no further analysis.

A file server disk image has been acquired for investigation. Using the image at /home/ubuntu/Downloads/SRL-2018/base-file-cdrive.E01, enumerate the partition layout, identify the Windows partition by offset, recover the full allocated directory tree, and extract a sorted list of deleted file entries from the root of the C: drive. Write all output to the case workdir.

The Rocba case folder at /home/ubuntu/Downloads/Standard-Forensic_Case/ contains a disk acquisition. Without mounting the image, enumerate the filesystem structure of the primary Windows partition, list all files under the Windows system directory, and identify any deleted artefacts in user profile directories that may indicate attacker activity.

Identify and analyse the disk image in /home/ubuntu/Downloads/SRL-2015/ to recover the partition table and directory listing of the primary Windows partition. If the requested artifact does not exist in that folder, reply with a single line beginning "ABSENT:" describing what you searched for, and perform no further analysis.

Investigate workstation 1 from the SRL-2018 case using the disk image at /home/ubuntu/Downloads/SRL-2018/base-wkstn-01-c-drive.E01. Extract the Master File Table and generate a timeline. Recover the Windows Security and System event logs and parse events relating to logon activity and service installation. Identify and parse any application prefetch files on the volume to establish an execution timeline.

The Rocba incident involved a Windows endpoint whose disk image is in /home/ubuntu/Downloads/Standard-Forensic_Case/. Extract Windows registry hives and programme execution artefacts from the image to reconstruct attacker persistence mechanisms and determine which user accounts were active during the incident window.

Search /home/ubuntu/Downloads/Standard-Forensic-Case-2/ for extracted Windows Security event log files to analyse authentication events and lateral movement indicators. If the requested artifact does not exist in that folder, reply with a single line beginning "ABSENT:" describing what you searched for, and perform no further analysis.

Generate a forensic super-timeline from the domain controller disk image at /home/ubuntu/Downloads/SRL-2018/base-dc-cdrive.E01. Include all available artefact parsers, normalise all timestamps to UTC, and produce a filtered view covering Windows System32 activity and user profile changes. Store the plaso storage file and filtered output in the case workdir.

Build a unified forensic timeline for the Rocba investigation using all available evidence sources in /home/ubuntu/Downloads/Standard-Forensic_Case/. Combine disk artefacts with any memory-derived timeline data to produce a coherent activity sequence. Filter the resulting timeline to highlight filesystem and registry events in the 48 hours surrounding the suspected compromise.

Build a forensic super-timeline from any disk image in /home/ubuntu/Downloads/SRL-2015/ that can be processed for filesystem, registry, and event log artefacts. If the requested artifact does not exist in that folder, reply with a single line beginning "ABSENT:" describing what you searched for, and perform no further analysis.

Conduct a full IOC sweep of the evidence in /home/ubuntu/Downloads/Standard-Forensic_Case/ including the memory image and any extractable file content. Write YARA rules targeting common post-exploitation indicators: packed PE headers, high-entropy payload sections, and known command-and-control string patterns. Test your rules against a clean reference directory before sweeping the evidence. Save all results following the standard export naming convention for this case.

Several compressed memory captures in /home/ubuntu/Downloads/SRL-2018/SRL-2018/ are suspected to contain injected shellcode stagers. Author YARA rules that detect PE headers embedded in unexpected memory regions and high-entropy payload blobs. In each rule condition, place lightweight structural checks before any computationally expensive entropy analysis. Run the rules against the available evidence archives and document hits.

Locate pre-written YARA rule files (.yar) stored in /home/ubuntu/Downloads/Standard-Forensic-Case-2/ and apply them to sweep the case evidence for malware signatures. If the requested artifact does not exist in that folder, reply with a single line beginning "ABSENT:" describing what you searched for, and perform no further analysis.

Scan process memory in /home/ubuntu/Downloads/Standard-Forensic_Case/Rocba-Memory/Rocba-Memory.raw for injected shellcode. Identify suspicious executable regions, dump any flagged segments for further analysis, and report the associated process names and PIDs.
